items.title: Monday morning’s premium across global markets has rapidly unwound. Traders are still on edge,…
items.content:
Monday morning’s premium across global markets has rapidly unwound. Traders are still on edge, with cross-asset implied volatility the highest since last April, but for some, there are reasons to hope the economic fallout from the war in the Middle East can be contained.

President Trump’s rhetoric played a big part in that. He suggested the war could end “very soon,” that US military objectives could be described as “pretty well complete” and called the conflict “a short-term excursion.”
In addition, he spoke directly to the energy price crunch, saying: “We’re looking to keep the oil prices down.” He discussed a potential waiver of certain oil-related sanctions, and is mulling a range options to combat surging prices, according to people familiar with the matter.
It’s not just Trump. French President Macron talked about a joint maritime mission to escort ships in an effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Group of Seven finance ministers said they’ll monitor the situation closely and stand ready to provide support, without feeling the need yet to release strategic oil reserves.
And in the Middle East, data from the UAE suggests Iran’s barrage of missiles and drones is easing, even as Tehran continues to carry out attacks. Some crude is still flowing and producers are also tapping ways to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.
A sustained shortfall of Middle East production would reduce crude and product stocks to their levels of a year ago within four to six weeks, according to a Societe Generale analysis. When inventories were previously at those levels, prices traded in a $75–$90/bbl range, according to the report. “More broadly, and at least on paper, the world has ample crude and product stocks to manage a severe disruption for several months.”
Policymakers around the world are meanwhile readying measures to manage the surging energy prices.

Of course, there are many caveats and the longer the conflict goes on without some of the above becoming reality, the more traders will start to price back in longer-term disruptions. And the less time they’ll have to pay attention to other major market themes like artificial intelligence and private credit.
週一早晨全球市場的風險溢價迅速消退。儘管跨資產隱含波動率仍處於去年四月以來最高水準，交易員依然緊張不安，但對某些人而言，中東戰爭對經濟造成的衝擊有望受到控制，這讓他們有理由抱持希望。Trump 總統的言論在其中發揮了重要作用。
他暗示戰爭可能「很快」結束，稱美國的軍事目標可以說「已大致完成」，並將這場衝突形容為「短期行動」。

此外，他直接談及能源價格危機，表示：「我們正設法壓低油價。」據知情人士透露，他討論了可能豁免部分與石油相關的制裁，並正在考慮一系列應對價格飆漲的選項。
這不僅是 Trump。法國總統 Macron 談及展開聯合海上任務以護送船隻，試圖重新開放 Strait of Hormuz（荷莫茲海峽）。G7（七大工業國集團）財政部長表示，他們將密切監控局勢，並準備好提供支援，但認為目前尚無釋出戰略石油儲備的必要。
而在中東地區，來自 UAE 的數據顯示，儘管德黑蘭持續發動攻擊，伊朗飛彈與無人機的密集攻勢正在趨緩。部分原油仍在流動，生產商也正設法繞過荷莫茲海峽。根據 Societe Generale 的分析，若中東產量持續短缺，原油與成品油庫存將在四至六週內降至一年前的水準。
根據該報告，當庫存先前處於該水準時，價格交易區間為每桶 75–90 美元。「更廣泛而言，至少在紙面上，全球擁有充足的原油與成品油庫存，足以應對數個月的嚴重供應中斷。」

各國政策制定者同時正準備相關措施，以應對飆升的能源價格。
當然，這其中存在許多但書，且衝突持續時間越長，上述情境未能成真的話，越多交易員將開始重新計入長期中斷的風險。而他們能夠關注其他重大市場主題的時間也會隨之減少，例如人工智慧與私募信貸。 @parisprogram

items.title: The market reaction to US President Donald Trump’s hints of an early end to the Iran war may pr…
items.content:
The market reaction to US President Donald Trump’s hints of an early end to the Iran war may prove to be too optimistic considering how much uncertainty remains.

European equities have gained over 2%, US futures have added to Monday’s upside and energy prices are declining. Bonds are flying, with the UK 2 year yield down 10bps as traders flip expectations for BOE action.

However, the magnitude of the moves jars with the reality of what has actually changed over the past 24 hours. Whilst Trump’s remarks about the war being resolved “very soon” are an incremental positive, there’s a lack of clarity over what comes next. The president stated that he didn’t believe the war would be over this week. His comments focusing on the need to lower energy prices appear more of an attempt to assuage market concerns. However, with the Strait of Hormuz still at a standstill and Middle Eastern oil producers slashing production, the efficacy of actions to reduce prices is highly uncertain.

The reaction today is indicative of a market that wishes to buy the dip at any available opportunity (as was the case last week), which is a clear positive for those carrying a bullish view beyond he conflict. However, the potential for much short-term disappointment remains. Some may be tempted to revisit the playbook from the 2025 trade war, which saw President Trump give way to market pressures. But Trump carries far less sway over the final outcome relative to last year’s disputes, given that current events are also subject to the actions of Israel and Iran, as opposed to a bilateral tariff negotiation.

Indeed, while there has been an improvement in expectations of a ceasefire in betting markets, odds of a resolution by the end of the month or next remain lacking in conviction.
市場對美國總統 Donald Trump 暗示伊朗戰爭可能提前結束的反應，考量到目前仍存在的高度不確定性，或許過於樂觀。歐洲股市已上漲超過 2%，美國期貨延續週一的漲勢，能源價格則下跌。債券市場表現強勁，英國 2 年期公債殖利率下滑 10 基點，因交易員調整對 BOE（英國央行）行動的預期。
然而，市場波動的幅度與過去24小時實際發生的變化並不相符。儘管川普關於戰爭將「很快」解決的言論帶來了一定程度的正面進展，但對於後續發展仍缺乏明確性。這位總統表示，他不認為戰爭會在本週結束。他強調需要降低能源價格的言論，看來更像是試圖緩解市場擔憂的舉措。
然而，隨著荷莫茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz）仍處於停滯狀態，且中東產油國大幅削減產量，降低油價相關措施的成效高度不確定。今日市場反應顯示，投資人希望把握任何可得的機會逢低買進（如同上週的情況），這對於在衝突之外持看多觀點的投資人而言是明確的正面訊號。然而，短期內出現大幅失望的可能性依然存在。
有些人可能會想重新採用 2025 年貿易戰的策略，當時 Trump 總統在市場壓力下做出了讓步。但相較於去年的爭端，Trump 對最終結果的影響力已大幅減弱，因為當前局勢不僅取決於雙邊關稅談判，還受到 Israel 與 Iran 行動的牽制。
確實，儘管押注市場對於停火協議的預期有所改善，但對於在本月底或下個月達成解決方案的機率仍缺乏信心。 @parisprogram

items.title: The krone will extend this year’s gains against the euro as above-target inflation and the spik…
items.content:
The krone will extend this year’s gains against the euro as above-target inflation and the spike in energy prices may spur an about-turn from Norway’s central bank.

Underlying inflation for February came in as forecast at 3%, suggesting that price pressures are still some way away from target. That, along with the current macroeconomic backdrop, has spurred traders to price out the earlier rate cut they were penciling in. While Norges Bank was reluctant to cut rates in a hurry even before the Iran war, the conflict may spur a shift in thinking, which will make its review later this month a watershed event.

It is possible that Norges Bank may abandon its pledge and even follow the Australian central bank’s lead in hiking rates. Should the European Central Bank be compelled later this year to raise rates, it is unlikely that Norges Bank will continue to loosen, narrowing the positive rate differential that Norway offers to investors.
Unlike most of its regional peers, Norway is a net energy exporter of natural gas and crude, so it stands to benefit from any increased flows due to higher prices, which will naturally support the krone.

While the krone is down versus the euro today, it’s strengthened almost 6% this year. These gains will accelerate if the Iran conflict draws to a quick close, as the krone has a higher beta to broader moves in the dollar. That means it stands to benefit to a greater degree than the euro.
挪威克朗兌歐元將延續今年以來的漲勢，因為高於目標的通膨以及能源價格飆升可能促使挪威央行政策轉向。2 月核心通膨率一如預期報 3%，顯示物價壓力距離目標仍有一段距離。這一情況加上當前的總體經濟背景，促使交易員將先前預期的提前降息排除在定價之外。
即使早在伊朗戰爭爆發前，Norges Bank 對於倉促降息仍持保留態度，但這場衝突可能促使其思維轉變，使得本月底的利率檢討成為關鍵轉折點。Norges Bank 有可能放棄其承諾，甚至效法澳洲央行（Reserve Bank of Australia）的作法進行升息。
若歐洲央行（ECB）在今年稍晚被迫升息，Norges Bank 不太可能繼續放寬貨幣政策，這將縮小挪威提供給投資者的正利差。與大多數區域同業不同，挪威是天然氣與原油的淨能源出口國，因此將受惠於價格上漲所帶動的資金流入增加，這自然會支撐挪威克朗。
儘管挪威克朗兌歐元今日走軟，但今年以來已升值近 6%。若伊朗衝突迅速落幕，這波漲勢將加速，因為挪威克朗對美元整體走勢的貝他值（beta）較高。這意味著其受益程度將高於歐元。 @parisprogram

items.title: ⚠️ The Swiss franc will stay aloft against the yen as the Bank of Japan falls behind the curve…
items.content:
⚠️ The Swiss franc will stay aloft against the yen as the Bank of Japan falls behind the curve in its fight against inflation.

CHF/JPY has surged past 200, in line with a case made here, essentially putting the cross into uncharted territory after an untiring rally that is now in its eighth year. The pace of gains may slow, as the Swiss National Bank is likely watching with a hawk’s eye given its long-stated wariness toward excessive currency strength.
However, the direction of travel is decidedly north for the cross. While the surge in energy prices will affect both economies, there is a crucial difference: unlike Japan, the majority of Switzerland’s crude imports are sourced from outside the Middle East.

The starting point matters, too: Switzerland had too little by way of inflation heading into the Iran war, unlike Japan, where the BOJ was already behind in taming price pressures. Now that energy prices have spiraled, Japan faces a higher import bill and those costs will cascade down the economy to worsen the nation’s inflation profile. With the BOJ still reluctant to step up its pace of policy tightening, the yen will be exposed in general against most currencies, as David Savage pointed out. That will be particularly so against the franc.
瑞郎兌日圓將持續走強，因日本央行在對抗通膨方面落後於形勢。CHF/JPY 已突破 200 關口，與此前在此提出的觀點一致，這場歷經八年、未曾停歇的漲勢，基本上已將該交叉匯率推入前所未見的領域。
漲勢可能趨緩，因為 Swiss National Bank 鑑於其長期以來對過度貨幣走強的警惕，很可能正密切關注。然而，該交叉匯率的走勢明確向上。儘管能源價格飆升將影響兩國經濟，但存在一個關鍵差異：與日本不同，瑞士大部分的原油進口並非來自中東地區。
起點也很重要：與日本不同，瑞士在伊朗戰爭爆發前的通膨水準過低，而日本則是 BOJ（日本央行）在抑制物價壓力方面早已落後。如今能源價格飆漲，日本面臨更高的進口成本，這些費用將在經濟體系中層層傳導，進一步惡化該國的通膨情勢。
日本央行（BOJ）仍不願加快其政策緊縮步伐，日圓兌大多數貨幣將普遍面臨貶值壓力，正如 David Savage 所指出的。兌瑞郎（franc）的情況尤其如此。 @parisprogram

items.title: Markets began the week focused on the risk of prolonged energy price increases before being whi…
items.content:
Markets began the week focused on the risk of prolonged energy price increases before being whipsawed by President Trump’s suggestion the conflict could end soon. Stocks are rallying today but little has changed fundamentally, with questions over the Strait’s reopening still unresolved. What has risen is macro uncertainty, lifting volatility that will weigh on global equities.

The impact of higher volatility works through three main channels. First, greater uncertainty raises the equity risk premium. As future cash flows become less predictable, investors demand higher compensation for risk, leading to valuation multiple compression, particularly in longer-duration growth assets. At the same time, larger price swings reduce risk-adjusted returns, making equity exposure less attractive.
Second, volatility can trigger mechanical de-risking. For example, risk parity strategies use volatility targeting, so when vol rises the amount invested in equities declines automatically. Higher hedging costs and margin requirements reinforce this adjustment.

Third, volatility tightens financial conditions. Wider credit spreads and a rising equity risk premium increase the cost of capital, curb issuance and buybacks, and weigh on confidence. In that sense, volatility acts as a form of passive tightening.

With no resolution to the conflict in Iran firmly in sight, volatility is likely to remain elevated. That persistence suggests another ongoing headwind for global equity markets.
市場本週開盤時聚焦於能源價格長期上漲的風險，隨後因 Trump 總統暗示衝突可能很快結束而劇烈波動。股市今日反彈，但基本面幾乎沒有改變，海峽重新開放的問題仍未解決。真正上升的是總體不確定性，推升波動性並將對全球股市造成壓力。波動性升高的影響主要透過三個管道發揮作用。
首先，不確定性升高會推升股票風險溢價。隨著未來現金流變得更難預測，投資人要求更高的風險補償，導致估值倍數壓縮，尤其是存續期較長的成長型資產。同時，較大的價格波動會降低經風險調整後的報酬，使得股票曝險的吸引力下降。其次，波動性可能觸發機械性的去風險操作。
舉例來說，風險平價策略採用波動率目標設定，因此當波動率上升時，投資於股票的資金會自動減少。較高的避險成本與保證金要求強化了這項調整。第三，波動率會緊縮金融環境。信用利差擴大與股票風險溢價上升會提高資金成本、抑制發行與庫藏股回購，並打擊市場信心。
從這個角度來看，波動性形同於一種被動的緊縮。由於伊朗衝突尚無明確的解決方案，波動性可能持續居高不下。這種持續性意味著全球股市將面臨另一股持續的不利因素。 @parisprogram

items.title: Global equity futures are continuing to pick up in early European trading, with the Eurostoxx 5…
items.content:
Global equity futures are continuing to pick up in early European trading, with the Eurostoxx 50 contract now up 2.2% and the S&P adding 0.2%. That’s come alongside another leg lower in crude and natural gas prices. Meanwhile, European bonds are rising, led by the periphery.

The same dynamics are being reflected in UK central bank pricing. BOE rate bets, which showed a near 90% chance of a hike this year at one point yesterday, now show a 50% chance of a cut.

As mentioned yesterday, the case for the BOE to tighten policy was already hampered by the weakness in the labour market compared to the 2022 Ukraine conflict and financial conditions that had began tightening. Indeed, there is already a behavioural element on display with UK consumers. Data from Barclays from a survey carried out after the conflict began showed that nearly half were “already taking action to adjust, for example by cutting energy use, spending less on discretionary items or saving more”.

As Ven noted though, the recovery isn’t absolute. German two-year yields have shed 6bps but still remain roughly 25bps above pre-conflict levels.

Gilts yields are set to open lower too, potentially even more so than German counterparts given the extent of yesterday’s moves. However, context does remain key in interpreting these moves.
全球股市期貨在歐洲早盤交易中持續上揚，Eurostoxx 50 期貨合約目前上漲 2.2%，S&P 則增加 0.2%。這與原油和天然氣價格再度下跌同步發生。同時，歐洲債券正在上漲，由邊緣國家領漲。同樣的動態也反映在英國央行定價中。
英國央行（BOE）的升息押注，昨日一度顯示今年升息機率接近 90%，如今則顯示降息機率為 50%。如昨日所述，相較於 2022 年烏克蘭衝突時期，英國勞動市場疲弱，加上金融環境已開始緊縮，這些因素早已削弱英國央行緊縮政策的理由。事實上，英國消費者行為已經出現變化。
根據 Barclays 在衝突爆發後進行的調查數據顯示，近半數受訪者「已經採取行動進行調整，例如減少能源使用、削減非必要支出或增加儲蓄」。然而正如 Ven 所指出的，復甦並非全面性的。德國兩年期公債殖利率已下跌 6 基點，但仍較衝突前水準高出約 25 基點。
英國公債殖利率預計也將開低，考量昨日漲幅之劇烈，跌幅甚至可能較德國公債更為顯著。然而，解讀這些走勢時，背景脈絡仍是關鍵。 @parisprogram

items.content: 起點也很重要：與日本不同，瑞士在伊朗戰爭爆發前的通膨水準過低，而日本則是 BOJ（日本央行）在抑制物價壓力方面早已落後。如今能源價格飆漲，日本面臨更高的進口成本，這些費用將在經濟體系中層層傳導，進一步惡化該國的通膨情勢。

items.title: 漲勢可能趨緩，因為 Swiss National Bank 鑑於其長期以來對過度貨幣走強的警惕，很可能正密切關注。然而，該交叉匯率的走勢明確向上。儘管能源價格飆升將影響兩國經濟，但存在一個關…
items.content: 漲勢可能趨緩，因為 Swiss National Bank 鑑於其長期以來對過度貨幣走強的警惕，很可能正密切關注。然而，該交叉匯率的走勢明確向上。儘管能源價格飆升將影響兩國經濟，但存在一個關鍵差異：與日本不同，瑞士大部分的原油進口並非來自中東地區。

items.title: 瑞郎兌日圓將持續走強，因日本央行在對抗通膨方面落後於形勢。CHF/JPY 已突破 200 關口，與此前在此提出的觀點一致，這場歷經八年、未曾停歇的漲勢，基本上已將該交叉匯率推入前所未見的領域。

items.content: 從這個角度來看，波動性形同於一種被動的緊縮。由於伊朗衝突尚無明確的解決方案，波動性可能持續居高不下。這種持續性意味著全球股市將面臨另一股持續的不利因素。

items.title: 舉例來說，風險平價策略採用波動率目標設定，因此當波動率上升時，投資於股票的資金會自動減少。較高的避險成本與保證金要求強化了這項調整。第三，波動率會緊縮金融環境。信用利差擴大與股票風險溢價上升…
items.content: 舉例來說，風險平價策略採用波動率目標設定，因此當波動率上升時，投資於股票的資金會自動減少。較高的避險成本與保證金要求強化了這項調整。第三，波動率會緊縮金融環境。信用利差擴大與股票風險溢價上升會提高資金成本、抑制發行與庫藏股回購，並打擊市場信心。

items.title: 首先，不確定性升高會推升股票風險溢價。隨著未來現金流變得更難預測，投資人要求更高的風險補償，導致估值倍數壓縮，尤其是存續期較長的成長型資產。同時，較大的價格波動會降低經風險調整後的報酬，使得…
items.content: 首先，不確定性升高會推升股票風險溢價。隨著未來現金流變得更難預測，投資人要求更高的風險補償，導致估值倍數壓縮，尤其是存續期較長的成長型資產。同時，較大的價格波動會降低經風險調整後的報酬，使得股票曝險的吸引力下降。其次，波動性可能觸發機械性的去風險操作。

items.content: 市場本週開盤時聚焦於能源價格長期上漲的風險，隨後因 Trump 總統暗示衝突可能很快結束而劇烈波動。股市今日反彈，但基本面幾乎沒有改變，海峽重新開放的問題仍未解決。真正上升的是總體不確定性，推升波動性並將對全球股市造成壓力。波動性升高的影響主要透過三個管道發揮作用。

items.content: 正如 George Lei 所指出的，選擇權持續顯示干預風險有限。與此同時，隨著日圓走軟，市場對 2026 年升息的預期已有所降溫，4 月升息的機率從約一個月前的近 80% 降至約 60%。若 OIS 交易員持續削減對 2026 年的升息押注，對通膨加速的擔憂可能對日圓造成更大壓力，使 USD/JPY 有機會測試 161 上方的週期高點。

items.content: 原油價格與日圓大致維持在週五收盤價位附近交易，即便週一曾出現劇烈波動。USD/JPY 現報 158 下方，該水準在 1 月日本央行進行利率檢查後曾被視為天花板，但隨著通膨展望惡化，現在看來可能已不具那麼強的約束力。能源價格上漲在短期內增添通膨壓力，同時惡化負擔能力、抑制成長，並使政策緊縮日益棘手。
